human_verification.notes: Reviewed Issue #1018 requirements for client-services-agency-specific workflow handling, evidence and confidentiality expectations, advisory, editorial, legal, contractual, financial, privacy, quality, and signoff boundaries, scenario completeness, functionality coverage, and validated packaging consistency.

review_notes: All required abilities across shared, service-support-and-resolution, and professional-and-information-services sets are declared and validated.

Scenario coverage includes normal client-service resolution, missing details, policy exceptions, legal/editorial handoff, repeat failures, confidentiality conflicts, unsupported workarounds, terminology handling, content workflow approval awareness, low-confidence facts, conflicting policies, and tenant adaptation.
Package remains specification-based and documents a separate promotion path for any future retained-implementation runtime.

specialist_owned_evidence: Outputs are bounded to agency client-service intake, status clarification, evidence-backed communication, missing-prerequisite handling, exception packaging, confidentiality handling, and owner-transfer handoff rather than legal, editorial, media strategy, financial, contractual, privacy, quality, or final signoff authority.

Source set includes IAB digital advertising terms, IAB/4A's media-buying terms, 4A's and ANA agency-client relationship guidance, FTC advertising claim and endorsement guidance, PRSA communication ethics, and ISO 10002 complaint-handling guidance with explicit refresh and decay posture.

Boundary logic explicitly prevents unsupported policy exceptions, hidden QA or approval blockers, unauthorized disclosure, unsupported advertising-claim approval, makegood or credit promises, and generic treatment of agency work without MSA, SOW, IO, project, content, approval, and communication context.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; non-blocking ambiguity is recorded as tenant, channel, platform, policy, confidentiality, approval, legal, editorial, privacy, financial, contractual, and authority prerequisites.

Runtime remains specification-based; retained-implementation promotion requires separate governance approval, telemetry evidence, retrieval wiring, communication safeguards, mutation safeguards, and non-regression validation.
